title: Oreogrammitis parva
intro: Oreogrammitis parva, also known as Small Shield Fern, is a species of fern native to tropical regions of Africa. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Oreogrammitis parva include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Oreogrammitis parva is commonly prepared as a tea or infusion, as well as being used in traditional remedies in the form of a paste or salve.
